seo_description: Current time in Santiago, Chile — live Chile Standard Time (CLT, UTC-4) clock, DST schedule, and time differences with Buenos Aires, New York, and world cities.

faq.question: What time is it in Santiago right now?
faq.answer: Santiago, Chile uses Chile Standard Time (CLT, UTC-4) in winter (May–August) and Chile Summer Time (CLST, UTC-3) during Daylight Saving Time in summer. Chile is in the southern hemisphere, so DST begins in October and ends in April. The IANA timezone identifier is America/Santiago.

faq.question: What time zone is Santiago in?
faq.answer: Santiago is in the Chile Standard Time zone (CLT, UTC-4 in winter; CLST, UTC-3 in summer). Chile observes DST in the southern hemisphere summer (October through April). The IANA identifier is America/Santiago. Note: Easter Island (part of Chile) uses a different timezone (Pacific/Easter, UTC-6/-5).

faq.question: Does Santiago observe daylight saving time?
faq.answer: Yes. Chile observes Daylight Saving Time. Because Santiago is in the southern hemisphere, clocks spring forward (to UTC-3, CLST) in October and fall back (to UTC-4, CLT) in April. Chile has historically adjusted its DST dates and may modify them based on energy policy decisions — always verify the current year's schedule.

faq.question: What is the time difference between Santiago and Buenos Aires?
faq.answer: Santiago (CLT, UTC-4 in winter) and Buenos Aires (ART, UTC-3 year-round) differ by 1 hour: Buenos Aires is 1 hour ahead of Santiago in winter. During Chile's DST (CLST, UTC-3), both are on the same offset. Argentina does not observe DST, so the gap oscillates between 0 and 1 hour.

faq.question: What is the time difference between Santiago and New York?
faq.answer: Santiago (CLT, UTC-4 in winter) is 1 hour ahead of New York (EST, UTC-5) in winter. During Chile's DST (CLST, UTC-3) and US DST (EDT, UTC-4), Santiago is still 1 hour ahead. The gap can briefly change during transition periods in spring and autumn.

faq.question: What is the time difference between Santiago and London?
faq.answer: Santiago (CLT, UTC-4 in winter) is 4 hours behind London (GMT, UTC+0) in UK winter. During UK summer (BST, UTC+1), the gap increases. During Chile's DST (CLST, UTC-3), Santiago is 3 hours behind London GMT. The opposing DST seasons create variable gaps.

faq.question: What are typical business hours in Santiago?
faq.answer: Standard business hours in Santiago are 9:00 AM to 6:00 PM CLT/CLST, Monday through Friday, with a 1–2 hour lunch break. Santiago is Chile's capital and economic hub, home to the Santiago Stock Exchange (Bolsa de Comercio de Santiago), which is one of Latin America's major exchanges. Major industries include copper mining, finance, and retail. Chile's economy is highly integrated with global copper markets.
